title: verbena xylopoda
intro: Verbena xylopoda, also known as the "wild verbena" or "Texas verbena", is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The medicinal properties of Verbena xylopoda contribute to its health benefits, including relieving congestion, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
